guide.title: Use one service workspace to keep continuity targets, dependencies, plans, and documents together
guide.summary: Continuity work is easier to maintain when the service record becomes the anchor for recovery planning and evidence.
body: Define the service, impact tier, recovery objectives, linked asset or risk, and ownership first.
Record dependencies and one or more recovery plans so the service can be reviewed as an operational whole.
Use workflow, plan details, and documents to keep the continuity posture current.

concepts.label: Continuity Service
concepts.summary: A business or technical service that the organization needs to keep running or recover quickly.
concepts.why_it_exists: Continuity work is easier to plan around services than around abstract obligations.
how_to_use: Register each important service and describe what it depends on.
Use recovery plans, exercises, and supporting documents to keep preparedness current.

relations.summary: Continuity services can list operational dependencies that matter to recovery.
A continuity service can have one or more recovery plans.

concepts.label: Service Dependency
concepts.summary: A recorded dependency that the service relies on for normal operation or recovery.
concepts.why_it_exists: Recovery planning is weaker when critical dependencies remain implicit.
how_to_use: Record the key dependency and its kind so recovery assumptions are explicit.
Review dependencies whenever the service architecture, supplier model, or fallback pattern changes.

relations.summary: Dependencies are maintained inside one service workspace.
A dependency may point to an asset or other operational component.

concepts.label: Recovery Plan
concepts.summary: A documented plan describing how to restore or maintain a service after disruption.
concepts.why_it_exists: A recovery plan turns continuity intent into actionable steps that can be reviewed and tested.
how_to_use: Write one plan per important recovery approach or fallback pattern.
Use linked policy, finding, test due dates, and owner assignments to keep the plan governed.

relations.summary: Plans are created to support one service.
